mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic strength and robust corporate earnings potential, leading to positive equity market returns. This is an always-on signal reflecting underlying economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market is historically correlated with bull markets.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from its recent low is a reliable leading indicator of a recession. The prospect of an imminent recession drives risk aversion and negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive median 60-day returns in the 12 months following more than 50% of trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: High and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a restrictive monetary policy (rising FEDFUNDS) creates a powerful headwind for equities. This interaction compresses valuation multiples and signals lower future corporate profits, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 4% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.50pp
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflation period showed this dynamic.
failure_condition: The model's predictions show a rank information coefficient below 0.0 over a 3-year rolling window when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, an unexpectedly strong real GDP (GDPC1) print is interpreted negatively by markets. It signals an overheating economy that will force the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy more aggressively, increasing discount rates and causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed 'good news is bad news' reaction function.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is below 4.5% and a GDP trigger occurs, median 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a significant slowdown in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy. This is a leading indicator of declining corporate earnings and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production downturns preceded NBER recessions.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce negative median 20-day returns in the quarter following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds a high threshold, the risk-free rate becomes highly competitive with the equity risk premium. This diverts capital from stocks to bonds and increases the discount rate on future earnings, causing negative pressure on the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity risk premium models show lower expected returns at high risk-free rates.
failure_condition: The median 60-day S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly lower (p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% compared to when it is below.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer spending, which is the primary driver of the US economy. This strength translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, boosting investor confidence and driving positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broad economic slowdown due to the sector's sensitivity to interest rates and its large economic multiplier. This signal of weakening aggregate demand leads to negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing downturns have preceded most post-WWII recessions.
failure_condition: In the six months following a trigger event, the S&P 500 has a positive median return across a sample of at least 5 such events.
